news_bbc.title: Next boss warns of 'dramatic' fall in entry-level jobs
news_bbc.summary: Lord Wolfson tells the BBC Next now typically receives double the number of applicants for one role than it did two years ago.

news_bbc.title: Social media as bad for young people as smoking, top doctors say
news_bbc.summary: The Academy of Medical Royal Colleges says doctors should routinely check on screen time and social media use when seeing younger patients.

news_bbc.title: Ferrari unveils first fully electric car
news_bbc.summary: The new Luce model has divided opinion on social media, and comes despite intense pressure from Chinese EV makers.

news_bbc.title: Oil prices slide on hopes of US-Iran peace deal
news_bbc.summary: Trump said on Saturday that an agreement would include the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, without giving further details.

news_bbc.title: Beer boom goes flat as breweries call last orders
news_bbc.summary: The UK's brewery scene is shrinking as pubs close, costs rise and drinking habits change.

news_bbc.title: The rise of the fruit that tastes like custard
news_bbc.summary: Custard apple plants are prized for their hardiness but exporting their delicate fruit is difficult.

news_bbc.title: 'I live in survival mode': The rise of the multi-job workforce
news_bbc.summary: More people are taking second jobs as rising costs and insecure work reshape how we earn a living.

news_bbc.title: How Saudi Arabia's spending spree reached the end of the line
news_bbc.summary: MBS's Vison 2030 project seemed the stuff of science fiction. Now reality has bitten
